collision_resolutions.conflict_description: MCUboot / ESP-IDF OTA bootloader uses internal heap allocations during firmware verification (mbedTLS SHA-256 streaming). This appears to violate PAT-002.
collision_resolutions.resolution: Scope PAT-002 to application firmware only (post-bootloader handoff). Bootloader heap use is bounded, single-phase, and not subject to long-running fragmentation. Document this as a PERMITTED_EXCEPTION with static buffer watermark assertion.

collision_resolutions.conflict_description: In Embedded Rust, volatile is not idiomatic. Using raw pointer volatile reads inside interrupt handlers conflicts with Rust's safe concurrency model (AtomicU32 preferred).
collision_resolutions.resolution: Language-conditional rule: In C, enforce volatile. In Rust, enforce core::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, AtomicBool} with Ordering::SeqCst or Ordering::Acquire/Release pair. Both satisfy the ISR determinism constraint.

collision_resolutions.conflict_description: Mutex cannot be acquired from ISR context. Using xSemaphoreTake from ISR causes undefined behavior in FreeRTOS.
collision_resolutions.resolution: ISR-to-task communication must use xQueueSendFromISR or xSemaphoreGiveFromISR exclusively. Mutex is application-task-to-application-task only. ISR acts as a producer; task acts as consumer/mutex holder.
